symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral proximal (shoulder/hip girdle) stiffness and pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric proximal girdle stiffness worse with rest is characteristic of PMR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal distal power/sensation; no bladder/bowel dysfunction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact distal neurology and continence argue strongly against cord compression.
Absence of cranial/visual features lowers, but given the overlap does not eliminate, GCA risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric proximal stiffness worse with rest/cold, no trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetric onset without trauma argues against a fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent next-day GP review with ESR/CRP and a corticosteroid trial
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: PMR is confirmed by the response to low-dose steroids; started by the GP, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent GCA work-up if cranial/visual features develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: GCA is a sight-threatening emergency requiring urgent steroids.
